skill.name: pexoai-agent
skill.description: AI video creation agent for short videos (5-120 seconds). Produces product ads, social content, brand videos, and explainers with professional cinematography.
skill.summary: Connects Claude to Pexo AI video platform for automated video production from text prompts and uploaded media.
skill.author: pexoai

security_audit.summary: Community skill for AI video production via Pexo API. Static analysis flagged 492 patterns (risk score 100) but most are false positives for legitimate shell scripting. Confirmed risks: requires PEXO_API_KEY environment variable, makes HTTPS requests to pexo.ai, uses shell scripts with command substitution for API orchestration. No malicious intent detected - all network calls go to documented Pexo endpoints, credential handling follows standard patterns, and file operations are limited to temp directories for API requests.

medium_findings.title: API Key in Environment Variable
medium_findings.description: Skill requires PEXO_API_KEY environment variable for authentication. This is standard practice for API integrations but users should ensure secure key management.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: Explicitly documented requirement for API authentication - standard pattern for SaaS integrations.

low_findings.title: External HTTPS Requests
low_findings.description: Makes authenticated HTTPS requests to pexo.ai API endpoints for video creation. All network traffic uses TLS encryption.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Confirmed legitimate API calls to documented Pexo endpoints with proper auth headers.

low_findings.title: Shell Command Substitution
low_findings.description: Uses $(...) syntax for variable assignment in shell scripts. Pattern detected by static analyzer but all commands are hardcoded utilities (curl, jq, stat, basename).
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Command substitution used for legitimate shell scripting - no user input injection vectors found.

low_findings.title: Temporary File Creation
low_findings.description: Creates temporary files using mktemp for HTTP request/response handling. Files are deleted after use.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard temp file pattern with proper cleanup - no persistence or sensitive data exposure.

low_findings.title: Config File in Home Directory
low_findings.description: Reads configuration from ~/.pexo/config file containing API credentials. Standard pattern for CLI tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Standard location for user-specific CLI configuration - follows Unix conventions.

content.user_title: Create AI videos from text prompts
content.value_statement: Transform text descriptions and media uploads into professional short videos using Pexo AI. Automates scriptwriting, shot composition, and editing without video production expertise.
actual_capabilities: Create 5-120 second videos from text prompts in multiple aspect ratios (16:9, 9:16, 1:1)
Upload and integrate images, videos, and audio files into video projects
Poll for video generation progress and handle preview options
Manage video revision requests using existing project IDs
Retrieve final video URLs with full CDN download links
limitations: Cannot crawl or process URLs - users must download files before uploading
Video generation takes 15-20 minutes for short videos
Requires valid PEXO_API_KEY and internet connection to pexo.ai
Each API call consumes tokens - consolidation of requests recommended

use_cases.title: Product Marketing Videos
use_cases.description: Create promotional videos for e-commerce products by uploading product photos and describing the desired style.

use_cases.title: Social Media Content
use_cases.description: Generate TikTok, Instagram Reels, and YouTube Shorts content from simple text briefs.
use_cases.target_user: Social media managers and content creators

use_cases.title: Explainer Videos
use_cases.description: Produce short explainer videos for features, tutorials, or onboarding by describing the topic.

prompt_templates.title: Basic video request
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a 30 second video of a cat playing with yarn in a living room setting

prompt_templates.title: Product video with upload
prompt_templates.prompt: Upload this product photo and create a promotional video showcasing the item from multiple angles with upbeat music

prompt_templates.title: Social media clip
prompt_templates.prompt: Make a vertical 9:16 video for Instagram Reels about morning coffee routines, 15 seconds, modern aesthetic

prompt_templates.title: Revision request
prompt_templates.prompt: The video looks good but can you make it more colorful and add faster transitions between scenes?

output_examples.input: Create a video of a sunset over mountains
output_examples.output: Video project created with ID proj_abc123. Your video is being generated and will be ready in 15-20 minutes. Project link: https://pexo.ai/project/proj_abc123

output_examples.input: Upload logo.png for the brand video
output_examples.output: File uploaded successfully. Asset ID: a_xyz789. The logo has been added to your project and will be included in the video.

best_practices: Always wrap uploaded asset IDs in proper tags like <original-image>asset_id</original-image> when sending to Pexo
Poll project status every 60 seconds during WAIT state - calling chat during WAIT causes duplicate videos
Send the full video download URL with all query parameters to users - truncated URLs return 403 errors
anti_patterns: Do not add duration, aspect ratio, or style details the user did not mention - Pexo handles these professionally
Do not call pexo-chat.sh during WAIT state - only poll with pexo-project-get.sh
Do not wrap video URLs in markdown links - send as plain text to avoid platform rendering issues

faq.question: How do I set up Pexo for the first time?
faq.answer: Create a config file at ~/.pexo/config with your PEXO_BASE_URL and PEXO_API_KEY. Run pexo-doctor.sh to verify the configuration.

faq.question: How long does video generation take?
faq.answer: Short videos (5-30 seconds) typically complete in 15-20 minutes. Longer or more complex videos may take additional time.

faq.question: What file types can I upload?
faq.answer: Images: jpg, png, webp, bmp, tiff, heic, heif. Videos: mp4, mov, avi. Audio: mp3, wav, aac, m4a, ogg, flac.

faq.question: Can I revise a video after it is generated?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the same project_id and send revision feedback to pexo-chat.sh. The skill will poll for the updated video.

faq.question: What aspect ratios are supported?
faq.answer: Three aspect ratios: 16:9 (landscape), 9:16 (vertical/portrait), and 1:1 (square). Pexo selects based on your request.

faq.question: Why is my video URL returning 403 Forbidden?
faq.answer: Ensure you copied the complete URL including all query parameters (OSSAccessKeyId, Expires, Signature). Truncated URLs will not work.
